channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Spidercam crashes onto pitch during Hungary match
item.description: A spider camera crashed near the touchline during a football friendly between Hungary and Kazakhstan.

item.title: Twelve killed in Johannesburg mass shooting, manhunt under way
item.description: Police say a manhunt is under way for more than 10 suspects after the attack on an informal settlement.

item.title: Baltic states fear Russia-Ukraine war spillover after drone incursions
item.description: A spate of drone incursions fuels anxieties about a possible military conflict with Russia.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 explained in maps and charts
item.description: Qualified teams, groups, schedule and snapshot of stadiums that will host World Cup matches.

item.title: Explosion in Bahrain as Iran attacks US bases
item.description: CCTV footage shows an explosion in Bahrain’s capital Manama after what authorities say was an Iranian missile strike.

item.title: Messi comes off the bench to score in Argentina’s final World Cup warm-up
item.description: Argentina ease past Iceland in their final friendly before the World Cup, winning 3-0 in Auburn, Alabama.

item.title: Ukrainian drones strike Sevastopol museum and key Russian oil refineries
item.description: The multiday campaign has killed a rail worker, closed the bombed Chongar road bridge and caused a fuel crisis

item.title: Iran war day 103: US strikes after helicopter shot down, Tehran hits back
item.description: Analysts warn of further escalation after tit-for-tat attacks between Tehran and Washington.

item.title: Indonesia jails four military officers for acid attack on rights activist
item.description: Prosecutors argued in court that the soldiers were motivated by anger over Yunus’s activism.

item.title: Prediction platform Kalshi to collect job details to combat insider trading
item.description: Prediction platform says it will seek employment information for markets at 'heightened' risk of manipulation.

item.title: Platner wins Maine Democratic Senate primary, vows to oust Collins
item.description: Platner wins Maine Senate primary despite controversies, setting up a key race against Republican Susan Collins.

item.title: Trita Parsi: Iran is pursuing a doctrine of swift retaliation
item.description: Iran has abandoned strategic patience for a new doctrine: swift, severe retaliation against any US attack.

item.title: Pope Leo welcomed in Barcelona with traditional Catalan human tower
item.description: More than 100 members of the Castellers de Vilafranca built a 10-metre-high human tower to welcome Pope Leo

item.title: Afghanistan says Pakistan air raids killed 13 people, including children
item.description: No immediate comment from Pakistan as Taliban says 11 children are among the dead in strikes on border provinces.

item.title: UN report: Israeli forces shield settlers during attacks on Palestinians
item.description: The UN says Israeli security forces routinely accompany settlers and act as a shield for their violence.

item.title: Jordan intercepts Iranian missiles over its airspace
item.description: Jordan intercepts Iranian missiles over its airspace

item.title: Somalia criticises US decision to bar World Cup referee
item.description: Somalia’s football federation says the US decision to deny entry to award-winning referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan

item.title: Air Canada pilot accused of flying for 17 years without proper licence
item.description: Former airline captain charged with fraud after allegedly commanding more than 900 flights without required credentials.

item.title: Bolivia approves military measures against nationwide protests
item.description: Bolivia approves military measures against nationwide protests.

item.title: Iran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan in retaliation for US strikes
item.description: Strikes come after US attacked Iranian ports and islands in the Strait of Hormuz over the downing of a helicopter.

item.title: Protesters torch cars, buildings in Belfast after knife attack
item.description: Unrest comes after a Sudanese man was arrested over a stabbing attack in north Belfast, UK.

item.title: Belfast plunged into chaos as vehicles set ablaze following stabbing attack
item.description: Al Jazeera's Milena Veselinovic reports from Belfast, where protests have escalated into violent riots.

item.title: South Korea’s booming stock market mints generation of novice investors
item.description: South Korean stocks are soaring, drawing first-time investors in a nation where property has long reigned supreme.

item.title: Brazil intercepts 108 Cuban immigrants amid growing asylum applications
item.description: Last year, for the first time in a decade, Cuban asylum applications exceeded Venezuelan ones in sign of growing strain.

item.title: Iran war live: Tehran targets US forces in Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait
item.description: Lebanon's Health Ministry says the death toll from Israeli attacks on the country since March 2 has reached 3,666.
